stories.title: Police, EMS respond to Durham gas station
stories.excerpt: Officers and medics were seen at a Han-Dee Hugo's along Miami Boulevard around 12 p.m.

stories.title: Charge: Durham man dragged Cary police officers who reached into his car
stories.excerpt: Damian Charles Awkward faces a total of seven charges, including two counts of assaulting a government official with a deadly weapon, felony possession of cocaine, misdemeanor resisting a public officer and misdemeanor possession of marijuana.

stories.title: NC man charged with felony child abuse, warrants describe malnutrition, developmental delays
stories.excerpt: The Durham County Sheriff's Office has charged a man with four counts of felony child abuse.

stories.title: Man killed along American Tobacco Trail in Durham, investigation underway
stories.excerpt: A man who was found critically injured Monday evening near the American Tobacco Trail has died, Durham police said. A homicide investigation is underway.

stories.title: Attempts to lower the rate of Black maternal deaths in NC face new challenges
stories.excerpt: The path leading to the track at a Durham middle school on Saturday was marked with signs bearing the names of Black women who died from pregnancy-related causes.  Walkers and runners participating in a fundraiser for MAAME, or a Durham nonprofit that offers doula care, walked past the signs,…

stories.title: Gun control advocates in NC work to preserve the permit law for concealed weapons
stories.excerpt: A bill allowing people to carry concealed handguns without permits is dangerous, Durham County Sheriff Clarence Birkhead said Tuesday, as it would increase gun violence and make the jobs of law enforcement officers harder.  Birkhead spoke at a news conference with gun control advocates. They…

stories.title: Record Numbers of Durham High Schoolers are Missing School
stories.excerpt: Over half of Durham Public Schools high schoolers were chronically absent in the first six months of the current school year.

stories.title: Former Durham City Council member’s lawsuit moves forward
stories.excerpt: A lawsuit filed by former Durham City Council Member Monique Holsey-Hyman is moving forward after an opinion by a state appellate court. The judges unanimously ruled to dismiss the appeal that cited legislative immunity for the former City Council members named in the suit.

stories.title: Fake federal court warrant nearly costs Durham resident $30K, sheriff warns
stories.excerpt: Durham sheriff warns scammers are impersonating federal courts with fake arrest warrants that look real.

stories.title: Durham schools prioritizing wellness to curb chronic absenteeism
stories.excerpt: Attendance in Durham Public Schools has not rebounded since the pandemic, with new district data showing that at least 37% of students are frequently missing class.

stories.title: Large outdoor party ends with 5 shot, 1 dead. Who is to blame?
stories.excerpt: Social media posts describing a Zebulon party where five people were shot suggested it was for North Carolina Central University students to celebrate the end of the semester.

stories.title: A Durham Death Doula’s Guide for Better Living and Dying
stories.excerpt: In a culture that will do anything to avoid talking about mortality, how should we think about death ? In a new book, Jane K. Callahan offers an  honest, empowering framework for preparing for the end.

stories.title: More Than 50 Years After Zoning Paved the Way for Development of Black Meadow Ridge, Half the Site Is Being Preserved
stories.excerpt: The Eno River Association helped spare 27 acres near West Point on the Eno from development. But the question remains: Why wasn’t Black Meadow Ridge protected to begin with?

stories.title: Housing, Eviction Diversion, Small Businesses: How Durham Has Allocated Pandemic Relief Money
stories.excerpt: The City of Durham has allocated more than $70 million in American Rescue Plan Act money.

stories.title: County Manager to Present FY2026-27 Budget on May 11
stories.excerpt: County Manager Claudia Hager will present the recommended FY2026-27 budget to the Board of Commissioners on May 11. A public hearing is scheduled for May 26 at 7 PM at 200 E. Main St. The Board is expected to adopt the budget at its June 8 meeting.

stories.title: Triangle Counties Come Out Against Proposed Cap on Local Property Taxes
stories.excerpt: Durham, Orange, and Wake counties warn that a potential constitutional amendment limiting property tax increases would jeopardize critical services.

stories.title: Feds Fine Durham-Based Energy Efficiency Company $722 Million
stories.excerpt: American Efficient says it incentivizes energy savings. One FERC commissioner claims “their entire business is a scam.”

stories.title: Earth Month Profile: Marc Maximov, Bike Durham
stories.excerpt: Longtime cyclist and Bike Durham board member Marc Maximov shares his roadmap for embracing life without a car.

stories.title: Budget Public Hearing: Have Your Say on May 26
stories.excerpt: Durham County will hold a public hearing on the FY2026-27 budget on May 26 at 7 PM in the Commissioners Chambers at 200 E. Main St. Neighbors can sign up to speak in person or submit written comments to the Board.

stories.title: It’s not rocket science: UNC System leaders make the case for increased funding
stories.excerpt: There’s perhaps no better advocate for North Carolina’s higher education system these days than NASA Astronaut Christina Koch. A mission specialist on Artemis II, Koch graduated from the North Carolina School of Science and Mathematics in Durham in 1997 and went on to North Carolina State…

stories.title: Young cancer survivor breaks ground on family housing for children in treatment at Duke Hospital
stories.excerpt: It would be an understatement to say that Harper Harrell has a smile that lights up a room — it lights up all of the outdoors. That was evident this week when the 12-year-old cancer survivor’s smile lit up her Durham backyard as she buzzed about, sharing with all comers her plans to build safe,…

stories.title: DPS Board Submits FY2026-27 County Funding Request
stories.excerpt: The Durham Public Schools Board of Education has submitted its FY2026-27 county appropriation request to the Durham County Board of Commissioners. The full recommended budget document is posted on the DPS Board of Education page.

stories.title: Applications Open for County Advisory Boards and Commissions
stories.excerpt: Durham County is accepting applications for vacancies on advisory boards and commissions, including the Planning Commission, Library Advisory Board, and others. Any Durham County neighbor can apply. Appointments are made by the Board of Commissioners.

stories.title: Two Durham elementary students detained by ICE, deported to Honduras within 48 hours
stories.excerpt: Two Durham elementary school students and their parents were detained by federal immigration authorities during a scheduled appointment in Charlotte earlier this week, and deported to Honduras within 48 hours, according to family members and advocates. The children, Genesis, 11, and Denis, 6,…

stories.title: BOCC Budget Work Sessions Begin in April
stories.excerpt: The Durham County Board of Commissioners is holding a series of budget work sessions throughout April and May to review departmental requests ahead of the County Manager's recommended budget presentation on May 11. Agendas and video recordings are posted on the BOCC agenda library page.
